raw:
reviewer hit max runtime 720000ms

I'll start by inspecting the repository and the changes.
Let me look at the full diff for the changed files.
Let me examine the planner.ts helpers and types referenced in the new function.
Let me look at the helper functions and PlannerPlan interface in detail.
Now let me look at the new `formatIntentContract` in context and compare with `renderFounderPlan` and `formatResolvedDecisions`.
Now let me examine the index.ts changes in context. Let me look at `formatResolvedDecisions` and `readPersistedPlannerDraft` first.
Now let me read the index.ts injection sites in full context.
Now let me see the round loop and how devContext is rebuilt across rounds, especially the resume branches and fail-retry.
Let me see the fail-retry path where devContext is rebuilt after a tester FAIL.
Let me examine the fail-retry rebuild at line 2106 and the other devContext reassignments at 1696, 1923, 2028.
The fail-retry rebuild at index.ts:2106 confirms the intent re-attach at the top of the loop (1605-1607) covers it. Let me verify the test fixture `understandingPlan` matches the new assertions.
Instructed not to rerun the suite. Let me verify the test harness setup and that existing tests are preserved.
Let me verify where `gate1Approved` is set and whether the round loop runs in the same invocation as approval (affecting whether the read at 1480 sees the persisted plan).
Let me check the unrelated-looking changes (README.md and log.jsonl files) for scope creep.
The README.md change is unrelated to this task (continual-learning extension). Let me check the log.jsonl changes and whether they're test-gate noise.
These are pre-existing working-tree noise (release-commit log appends from other tasks and an unrelated continual-learning README edit), not part of this task. Let me verify no other test greps the comment/strings I changed, and check the full test suite for regressions caused by the edits.
Let me verify crew_escalation_test.sh greps still hold against the modified code (it greps for the exact decisions strings which still exist).
Let me check no existing test greps the old comment/string I changed, and verify the budget/reviewer tests aren't coupled to the testerTask structure.
Let me verify no other tests grep the crew prompt lines or testerTask shape that my changes could break, and check the legacy-path gating.
Let me verify the Gate 1 block guard and whether legacy path can have gate1Approved set, to confirm legacy behavior is truly unchanged.
Let me check how Gate 1 is handled for the legacy no-foreman.json path â€” whether gate1Approved gets auto-set, affecting the read.
Let me verify the fallback plan doesn't populate the understanding-layer fields (back-compat: should inject nothing).
Let me verify the crew prompt files are intact (frontmatter/model lines) and the additions read cleanly.
Let me confirm planner_test.sh has only additions (no deleted tests) and check the reviewer/budget tests are untouched.
Let me verify the other devContext rebuild sites (1923, 2028) continue the loop so intent re-attaches.
Let me confirm the remaining devContext rebuild sites continue the loop (so intent re-attaches) and that no existing test assertions were removed.
